theatres.name: Hab-Stack Warrens
theatres.description: Towering hab-blocks crammed with thousands of desperate souls, connected by rusting gantries and crumbling walkways. When gang war erupts here, it becomes a grinding close-quarters fight through endless corridors and cramped chambers.
theatres.tacticalBenefits: These Twists provide a boost to units in cover, increasing their survivability. Well suited to gangs with large numbers of fighters that can take advantage of dense terrain.
theatres.recommendedTerrain: Use a mixture of ruins and buildings placed evenly across the battlefield to represent the cramped hab-blocks.

twists.name: Rolling Fumes
twists.flavourText: Thick, choking smoke from burning refuse and ruptured promethium lines fills the narrow passages.
twists.rule: Models in cover gain Stealth. Models with Stealth get an additional +1 to Defense rolls when targeted by shooting attacks from more than 12" away.

twists.name: Firing Positions
twists.flavourText: Every doorway and window becomes a potential killzone. Gangers set up ambush points with practiced efficiency.
twists.rule: Models in cover gain Ambush. Models with Ambush that did not move this activation get +1 to hit with shooting attacks.

twists.name: Maintenance Tunnels
twists.flavourText: A labyrinth of service ducts and forgotten access corridors allows gangers to appear where least expected.
twists.rule: Once per round, after a model finishes its activation while in cover, its controlling player may immediately place it in cover anywhere within their deployment zone.

theatres.name: The Sump
theatres.description: The lowest depths where toxic runoff pools and desperate scavengers pick through industrial waste. Fighting here means navigating treacherous footing and lethal environmental hazards.
theatres.tacticalBenefits: These Twists help weaker fighters take down tougher targets using environmental hazards and improvised explosives.
theatres.recommendedTerrain: Use pipes, containers, barrels and barricades as well as scattered industrial debris to reflect salvage operations and jury-rigged defenses.

twists.name: Unstable Munitions
twists.flavourText: Abandoned ordnance and volatile chemicals litter the area, ready to be triggered by a well-placed shot.
twists.rule: At the start of each round, place 3 Explosive markers in no man's land. Any model may target an Explosive marker (Defense 4+). If hit, all models within 6" take a hit with AP(1). Remove the marker after detonation.

twists.name: Crumbling Platforms
twists.flavourText: The corroded gantries and walkways groan under the weight of combat, threatening to collapse at any moment.
twists.rule: When a model takes a wound while on elevated terrain, roll a die. On a 1-2, that model falls and takes an additional hit with AP(1).

twists.name: Toxic Spray
twists.flavourText: Pressurized pipes full of corrosive chemicals make tempting targets for those willing to weaponize the environment.
twists.rule: All shooting attacks gain Poison. Poison attacks wound on 2+ regardless of target's Tough value, but only against targets within 12".

theatres.name: The Docks
theatres.description: Cargo transit hubs where goods flow in and out of the hive. Control of the docks means control of supplies, making them fiercely contested territory.
theatres.tacticalBenefits: These Twists make holding objectives easier and improve mobility, helping elite gangs avoid getting bogged down.
theatres.recommendedTerrain: Use cargo containers, loading cranes, and warehouse structures with gaps wide enough for movement.

twists.name: Lockdown Protocol
twists.flavourText: Securing cargo bays triggers automated systems that seal bulkheads and restrict access.
twists.rule: When a model ends its activation holding an objective, that objective counts as held by that player even if the model moves away, until an enemy model holds it.

twists.name: Surveillance Grid
twists.flavourText: Hacking into the dock's pict-feeds provides invaluable intelligence on enemy movements.
twists.rule: Models gain Scout. Scout models may be deployed up to 12" outside their deployment zone. Additionally, models that Fall Back may still shoot this activation at -1 to hit.

twists.name: Cargo Lifts
twists.flavourText: Industrial elevators and cargo movers allow rapid repositioning across the dock complex.
twists.rule: Models within 3" of an objective marker may spend their activation to be placed within 3" of any other objective marker. They may not take any other actions that activation.

theatres.name: Chem-Spill Sector
theatres.description: A blighted zone where industrial accidents have left the air thick with toxins and the ground saturated with mutagenic chemicals. Only the desperate or the foolhardy fight here.
theatres.tacticalBenefits: These Twists reduce shooting effectiveness and improve survivability, favoring attritional gangs.
theatres.recommendedTerrain: Use terrain that limits visibility, with structures huddled in more sheltered areas.

twists.name: Toxic Cloud
twists.flavourText: Clouds of corrosive vapor drift unpredictably, reducing visibility and fouling targeter systems.
twists.rule: Round 1-2: Maximum shooting range is 24". Round 3-4: Maximum shooting range is 18". Round 5+: Maximum shooting range is 12".

twists.name: Chem-Burns
twists.flavourText: Prolonged exposure to the caustic atmosphere weakens even the toughest fighters, leaving them sluggish and vulnerable.
twists.rule: At the start of each round after the first, all models not in cover take a hit with AP(0). Models with Tough reduce their Tough value by 1 for the duration of the game (minimum 1).

twists.name: Interference Storm
twists.flavourText: Electromagnetic discharges from unstable chemicals disrupt communications and targeting systems.
twists.rule: Models cannot use special abilities that require targeting allies. All shooting attacks suffer -1 to hit. The player who deploys second gains 1 bonus command point.

theatres.name: Factorum District
theatres.description: Abandoned manufacturing plants where rusting machinery still groans and sparks. The forges may have cooled, but the hazards remain - volatile fuel lines, unstable structures, and choking industrial fumes.
theatres.tacticalBenefits: These Twists aid tough, durable models while punishing lighter fighters with environmental damage.
theatres.recommendedTerrain: Use industrial terrain elements - machinery, pipes, and manufacturing equipment.

twists.name: Corrosive Vapour
twists.flavourText: Clouds of acidic steam vent from cracked pipes, eating through armor and flesh alike.
twists.rule: All attacks against targets within 6" gain AP(1). Models without Tough that are wounded in melee take an additional wound on a roll of 5+.

twists.name: Fuel Line Rupture
twists.flavourText: Damaged fuel conduits build pressure until they detonate with terrifying force.
twists.rule: At the start of round 2 and round 4, roll a die for each model within 6" of terrain. On a 1, that model becomes Stunned and skips its next activation.

twists.name: Scorching Floors
twists.flavourText: Heat radiates from cooling forges and slag pools, making footing treacherous.
twists.rule: Models that Advance (move more than their base move) take a hit with AP(0) at the end of their activation. Models with Tough are immune to this effect.

theatres.name: Burnout Zone
theatres.description: Sectors gutted by fire, riot, or gang warfare. Nothing remains but ash-covered rubble and the charred shells of former structures. Wide open and merciless.
theatres.tacticalBenefits: These Twists benefit larger models and vehicles with increased mobility and durability in open terrain.
theatres.recommendedTerrain: Cluster terrain features together with wide gaps between them for increased movement.

twists.name: Open Ground
twists.flavourText: With no cover to impede them, heavier assets surge across the devastation.
twists.rule: Models with Tough(3) or higher gain Fast. Fast models add 2" to their movement. All other models suffer -1 to Defense when not in cover.

twists.name: Rubble Piles
twists.flavourText: Collapsed walls and debris mounds provide elevated firing positions for those heavy enough to climb them.
twists.rule: Models with Tough(3) or higher that did not move this activation gain +1 to hit with shooting attacks and may re-roll one failed hit roll.

twists.name: Ash Clouds
twists.flavourText: Movement kicks up choking clouds of ash and debris, providing concealment on the move.
twists.rule: Models with Tough(3) or higher that moved this activation gain Stealth until their next activation. Stealth provides +1 to Defense against shooting from more than 12" away.

theatres.name: The Undercroft
theatres.description: Ancient tunnels and forgotten chambers beneath the hive, predating the current structures by millennia. Strange technologies and older dangers lurk in these depths.
theatres.tacticalBenefits: These Twists are unpredictable and chaotic, creating varied and interesting battles.
theatres.recommendedTerrain: Mix standard terrain with unusual pieces to represent ancient architecture.

twists.name: Unstable Passages
twists.flavourText: The ancient corridors shift and buckle, hurling combatants toward one another unexpectedly.
twists.rule: All models add +D6" to their movement value. However, if they roll a 1 on this die, they become Shaken and suffer -1 to hit until their next activation.

twists.name: Ancient Tech
twists.flavourText: Glowing devices of unknown origin pulse with strange energies, disrupting modern equipment.
twists.rule: All weapons gain +6" range. However, each time a model shoots, roll a die. On a 1, that model takes a hit with AP(2) from energy feedback.

twists.name: Protective Fields
twists.flavourText: Shimmering barriers of unknown energy flicker across the battlefield, deflecting incoming fire.
twists.rule: All models gain a 5+ invulnerable save against shooting attacks. This save is taken after the normal Defense roll fails.

theatres.name: Slag Heaps
theatres.description: Mountains of industrial waste and discarded refuse, constantly shifting and unstable. Scavengers pick through the debris while gangs fight for control of valuable salvage.
theatres.tacticalBenefits: These Twists favor infantry models, enabling them to outmaneuver heavier opponents.
theatres.recommendedTerrain: Use similar-sized terrain features scattered through no man's land to represent refuse piles and salvage camps.

twists.name: Treacherous Footing
twists.flavourText: The unstable ground shifts constantly, favoring lighter and more agile fighters.
twists.rule: Models without Tough gain +2" movement and +1 to charge rolls. Models with Tough(3) or higher suffer -2" movement and -1 to charge rolls.

twists.name: Scrap Cover
twists.flavourText: Piles of refuse provide ample concealment for those who know how to use them.
twists.rule: Models without Tough may deploy using Ambush (deploy after seeing enemy deployment, up to 12" from any enemy). Models with Ambush gain +1 to their first shooting attack.

twists.name: Junk Barricades
twists.flavourText: Improvised fortifications of scrap metal and debris provide surprisingly effective cover.
twists.rule: Models without Tough that are in cover gain +2 to Defense instead of +1. Heavy weapons (weapons with Blast or AP(3+)) suffer -1 to hit.

theatres.name: Fungal Depths
theatres.description: Sections of the underhive where mutant fungi and toxic growths have taken over, creating a choking maze of spore-clouds and grasping tendrils. Only the most desperate venture here.
theatres.tacticalBenefits: These Twists boost melee-oriented gangs with improved damage and punishment for those who retreat.
theatres.recommendedTerrain: Use organic-looking terrain and ruins to represent areas overrun with mutant growth, scattered across the battlefield.

twists.name: Rage Spores
twists.flavourText: Inhaling the drifting spores drives fighters into a frenzy of violence.
twists.rule: All models gain Furious. Furious models get +1 attack in melee when they charge. Additionally, models that kill an enemy in melee may immediately move 3" toward the nearest enemy.

twists.name: Strangling Growth
twists.flavourText: Grasping tendrils and toxic thorns make retreat a deadly proposition.
twists.rule: Models that Fall Back take a hit with AP(1) from the grasping vegetation. Models that Fall Back cannot shoot or charge for the rest of that activation.

twists.name: Carnivorous Flora
twists.flavourText: Maw-plants, toxin-spitters, and acid-drippers lurk throughout, waiting for unwary prey.
twists.rule: At the end of each round, each model not in base contact with a friendly model takes a hit with AP(0) from the predatory plants. Models in melee are immune as the plants avoid the struggle.
